title: RBI Monetary Policy: Repo Rate Unchanged at 6.5%, Stance Remains 'Withdrawal of Accommodation'
summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee has decided to maintain the repo rate at 6.5% for the tenth consecutive time. This decision aims to balance inflation management with economic growth stability while keeping a close eye on global economic uncertainties and domestic price pressures.
content: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor Shaktikanta Das announced the decision of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%. This move was largely expected by market analysts and economists. The Governor highlighted that while inflation is on a downward trajectory, it remains above the target level of 4%. The MPC's primary focus remains the withdrawal of accommodation to ensure that inflation progressively aligns with the target while supporting growth.
whyMatters: The steady interest rate environment provides predictability for businesses and retail borrowers.
shortTermImpact: Neutral for markets; relief for banking sector as no hike was announced.
longTermImpact: Supports steady credit growth and helps in long-term inflation anchoring.
investorTakeaway: Maintain exposure to quality private banks. Watch for the next inflation data release.

title: Reliance Industries Q3 Results: Profit Jumps 12% YoY, Jio AirFiber Subscriptions Hit New Milestone
summary: Reliance Industries has reported a significant 12% year-on-year increase in quarterly profit, bolstered by strong performance in its digital and retail sectors. The success of Jio AirFiber has been a key driver, marking a pivotal moment in the company's aggressive expansion into home broadband services.
content: Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) reported its third-quarter financial results, showcasing a 12% increase in net profit year-on-year. The growth was primarily driven by the stellar performance of its digital services arm, Jio, and its retail division. Jio reported a healthy increase in Average Revenue Per User (ARPU).
whyMatters: Reliance is a market heavyweight; its performance dictates the movement of Nifty 50.
shortTermImpact: Stock likely to see 2-3% gap up opening. Positive sentiment for O2C and Retail segments.
longTermImpact: Expansion of digital ecosystem ensures long-term cash flow stability and valuation rerating.
investorTakeaway: Core portfolio holding. Buy on dips for long-term compounding.
